instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
GST on Term Insurance
Getting life cover just became a lot lighter on the pocket. With the rollout of the new GST 2.0 rules, the GST payable on term insurance premiums has been reduced from 18% to 0%, starting 22 September 2025. For many people, that old 18% tax on premiums pushed the cost of life insurance plans. With term insurance now being more affordable, it encourages more people to secure their family’s future without the GST implication. Let’s understand this a little better.
What is Term Insurance?
Term insurance is the simplest way to secure your family financially. You pay a premium for a specific premium payment term, and if something happens to you during the policy tenure, your family gets a death benefit. If you outlive the policy term, no maturity benefit is usually paid under term plans. However, if you have chosen the term plan with return of premium, the premiums paid are refunded.
Since term insurance is a protection-oriented life insurance plan, the premiums are affordable.
GST Payable on Term Insurance Premiums
GST (Goods and Services Tax) on insurance is a type of indirect tax applied to the services provided by insurers. Until recently, this tax increased the overall premium you paid for a term plan because insurers added GST to the base price.
Following the 56th GST Council meeting in September 2025, premiums on individual term plans were exempted from GST. The aim was to make life protection cost-effective and accessible for all.
Earlier, buying a term insurance meant paying 18% GST on term plan. Now, you pay only the base premium, which significantly reduces your cost. Here’s how the change looks when you compare numbers:
Note: Group life cover, group employer-provided term plans, and corporate life insurance still attract GST.
Benefits of the Recent GST Reforms in the Context of Term Insurance
Here are some benefits of zero GST rates on term insurance:
Reduction in Premium Rates for Individual Term Plans
With GST removed, your yearly payout drops instantly. For example, say your premium is ₹15,000, you earlier paid ₹2,700 extra in tax. Now you save that full amount every year, which means you can save ₹54,000 for a 20-year policy term. This benefit becomes more meaningful in term plans with higher sum assured levels.
Improved Accessibility to Insurance Plans Enabling Financial Inclusion
With the GST on term plan being removed, more households can finally afford proper life cover at a low premium. This shift encourages people to consider insurance as a growing necessity rather than just a tax-saving tool. It also makes adoption easier in rural and semi-urban areas, where even small savings can make a big difference in choosing formal financial protection.
Improved Affordability of Term Plan Riders
Simple Structure of Premium and Easier Taxation
With GST removed, premiums are easier to understand and genuinely lower. Anyone using a term insurance calculator can clearly see the reduced cost, which gives more confidence to individuals who earlier hesitated due to the extra tax load.
Supports the Insurance Goals of the Country and Provides Financial Security
The push towards 0% GST on life insurance aligns with      India’s broader goal of expanding insurance reach under IRDAI’s Vision 2047. The mission is to make essential protection accessible to every citizen. The goal is to build a strong insurance sector in India and ultimately position it among the world’s leading providers of financial security.
GST Removal on Term Insurance Premiums
The removal of GST on term insurance isn’t just a tax cut, but a shift that can reshape how people approach life protection in India. Here’s what the change means for you:
Cost relief: Term plans are instantly cheaper since the earlier 18% tax no longer applies.
Better affordability: More households can now fit life cover into their budgets without hesitation.
Stronger financial security: With reduced costs, insurance penetration is expected to rise, helping families stay protected during income disruptions.
Supports long-term planning: Affordable term cover makes it easier to integrate insurance with savings and investment goals, creating a more rounded financial plan.
Frees up extra money for other priorities: The amount saved can be redirected to important goals like retirement planning, children’s education, or building an emergency fund.
Higher sum assured: You can now opt for a larger life cover without increasing your premium. Instead of losing money to GST on term insurance, that amount can be used to boost your protection.
How GST Reforms Would Affect Policyholders?
Since term insurance premiums have been made GST-exempt, effective 22nd September, 2025, policyholders will pay lower premiums. If your annual premium was ₹10,000, you were actually paying ₹11,800 (₹10,000 + ₹1,800 GST). Now you can save this ₹1,800 every year. This value increases for higher premium amounts. Thus, people from all economic sections can purchase a term plan for their family’s security.
Benefits Available to Existing Customers
If you already have a term plan, you’ll also enjoy the GST relief, as your future premiums will automatically reduce.
If your policy had lapsed before 22.09.2025 but you revive it on or after that date, the premiums you pay after reinstatement will also be free from GST on term insurance.
Impact of GST Reforms on Insurers
Here’s how the latest GST will affect the insurers:
Growth in Sales
With no GST applicable on term insurance premiums, affordability improves, and as a result, more people might consider purchasing a policy. This uplift in sentiment can drive higher demand across different age and income groups. It’s a good opportunity for insurers to widen protection coverage, although they may need to refine processes and marketing to handle the increased interest smoothly.
Comparison Between Current GST and Proposed GST Reforms on Term Insurance
With the withdrawal of GST rates on term insurance, pure protection plans will be cheaper and easier to buy after September 22, 2025. However, group term insurance will be taxed at 18% GST. Below is a comparison of how things change:
Key Takeaways
Effective from September 22, 2025, GST on term insurance is now 0% for individual plans, making premiums noticeably cheaper.
Policyholders get more value for the same budget, allowing higher cover without extra cost.
The reform supports national financial goals by improving access to basic life protection.
People from rural and semi-urban areas can buy a term insurance easily without a tax burden.
Existing customers also benefit, as future premiums will automatically reduce.
Insurers may face higher operating costs, but overall demand for term plans is expected to rise.
Conclusion
The withdrawal of GST on term insurance is a big step in making protection more affordable for every Indian. It puts real savings back into your pocket and encourages more people to secure long-term cover. For insurers, it signals stronger growth and wider reach, bringing India’s goal of “Insurance for All” closer to reality. If you’ve been waiting to buy a term plan, this is the right moment to lock in low premiums and protect your family’s future.
FAQs
Is the GST change relevant for individual term plans and group term plans alike?
The GST waiver is limited to individual term insurance plans. Premiums of group term insurance plans will include 18% GST even under the GST 2.0 reform.
Can policyholders claim a GST refund on term insurance premiums already paid for several years?
Refunds aren't allowed for GST that was charged on earlier payments.The new GST exemption applies only when two out of three events: service period, invoice date, or payment date, fall on September 22, 2025 or after this date. If two of these events happened before that date, the old GST rate continues to apply.
How to Calculate GST on Term Insurance?
Before GST 2.0, the premium amount of individual and group plans attracted 18% GST. This means that an annual premium of ₹10,000 attracted an additional tax of ₹1,800, bringing the total premium amount to ₹11,800. After the reform, a premium of ₹10,000 on individual term plan will not incur any extra GST charges.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility

GST on Term Insurance
Getting life cover just became a lot lighter on the pocket. With the rollout of the new GST 2.0 rules, the GST payable on term insurance premiums has been reduced from 18% to 0%, starting 22 September 20251. For many people, that old 18% tax on premiums pushed the cost of life insurance plans. With term insurance now being more affordable, it encourages more people to secure their family’s financial future. Let’s understand this a little better.
What is Term Insurance?
Term insurance is the simplest way to secure your family financially. You pay a premium for a specific premium payment term, and if something happens to you (the life assured) during the policy tenure, your nominee gets the death benefit. If you outlive the policy term, no maturity benefit is usually paid under term plans. However, if you have chosen the term plan with return of premium, the premiums paid are refunded.
GST Payable on Term Insurance Premiums
GST (Goods and Services Tax) on insurance is a type of indirect tax applied to the services provided by insurers. Until recently, this tax increased the overall premium you paid for a term plan because insurers added GST to the base price.
Following the 56th GST Council meeting in September 2025, premiums on individual term plans were exempted from GST. The aim was to make life insurance cost-effective and accessible for all.
Earlier, buying a term insurance meant paying 18% GST on term plan. Now, you pay only the base premium, which significantly reduces your cost. Here’s how the change looks when you compare numbers:
Scenario Base Premium (₹) (Assumed)
GST Rate GST Amount (₹) Total Payable (₹)
Old Regime (Before 22 Sept’25)
20,000 18 3,600 23,600
New Regime 20,000 0 0 20,000
Note: Group life cover, group employer-provided term plans, and corporate life insurance still attract GST.
Benefits of the Recent GST Reforms in the Context of Term Insurance
Here are some benefits of zero GST rates on term insurance:
1. Reduction in Premium Rates for Individual Term Plans
With GST removed, your yearly payout drops instantly. For example, say your premium is ₹15,000, you earlier paid ₹2,700 extra in tax. Now you save that full amount every year, which means you can save ₹54,000 for a 20-year policy term. This benefit becomes more meaningful in term plans with higher sum assured levels. 2. Improved Accessibility to Insurance Plans Enabling Financial Inclusion
With the GST on term plan being removed, more households can finally afford proper life cover at a low premium. This shift encourages people to consider insurance as a growing necessity rather than just a tax-saving tool. It also makes adoption easier in rural and semi- urban areas, where even small savings can make a big difference in choosing formal financial protection. 3. Simple Structure of Premium and Easier Taxation
With GST removed, premiums are easier to understand and comparatively lower. Anyone using a term insurance calculator can clearly see the reduced cost, which gives more confidence to individuals who earlier hesitated due to the extra tax load. 4. Supports the Insurance Goals of the Country and Provides Financial Security
The push towards 0% GST on life insurance aligns with India’s broader goal of expanding insurance reach under IRDAI’s Vision 2047. The mission is to make essential protection accessible to every citizen. The goal is to build a strong insurance sector in India and ultimately position it among the world’s leading providers of financial security.
GST Removal on Term Insurance Premiums
The removal of GST on term insurance isn’t just a tax cut, but a shift that can reshape how people approach life protection in India. Here’s what the change means for you:
1. Cost relief: Term plans are instantly cheaper since the earlier 18% tax no longer applies. 2. Better affordability: More households can now fit life cover into their budgets without
hesitation. 3. Stronger financial security: With reduced costs, insurance penetration is expected to rise,
helping families stay protected during income disruptions. 4. Supports long-term planning: Affordable term cover makes it easier to integrate insurance
with savings and investment goals, creating a more rounded financial plan. 5. Frees up extra money for other priorities: The amount saved can be redirected to important
goals like retirement planning, children’s education, or building an emergency fund. 6. Higher sum assured: You can now opt for an optimal life cover affordably.
How GST Reforms Would Affect Policyholders?
Since term insurance premiums have been made GST-exempt, effective 22nd September, 2025, policyholders will pay lower premiums. If your annual premium was ₹10,000, you were actually paying ₹11,800 (₹10,000 + ₹1,800 GST). Now you can save this ₹1,800 every year. This value increases for higher premium amounts. Thus, people from all economic sections can purchase a term plan for their family’s security.
1. Benefits Available to Existing Customers
If you already have a term plan, you’ll also enjoy the GST relief, as your future premiums will automatically reduce.
If your policy had lapsed before 22.09.2025 but you revive it on or after that date, the premiums you pay after reinstatement will also be free from GST on term insurance2.
Impact of GST Reforms on Insurers
Here’s how the latest GST will affect the insurers:
1. Growth in Sales
With no GST applicable on term insurance premiums, affordability improves, and as a result, more people might consider purchasing a policy. This uplift in sentiment can drive higher demand across different age and income groups. It’s a good opportunity for insurers to widen protection coverage, although they may need to refine processes and marketing to handle the increased interest smoothly.
Comparison Between Previous GST and GST 2.0 Reforms on Term Insurance
With the withdrawal of GST rates on term insurance, pure protection plans will be cheaper and easier to buy after September 22, 2025. However, group term insurance will be taxed at 18% GST. Below is a comparison of how things change1:
Category GST before 22 Sept 2025 After GST 2.0 GST Applied 18% on all term plans 0% on individual term plans; 18% on group term plans, group employer- provided term plans Premium Amount
Premiums were comparatively more since GST was added
Premiums drop instantly as GST is removed for individuals Cost to Policyholders
Buyers pay an added tax over the base premium
No GST charged, so individuals pay only the base premium Who is Impacted
Both individual term plans and group term plans are taxed alike.
Only individual policyholders get the benefit; group plans stay taxed
Start Date GST applicable @18% starting from July 2017
New 0% GST applies with effect from September 22, 2025 Overall Advantage
GST applicability increased term insurance premiums
Lower premiums make life insurance plans more appealing and cost effective Illustration ₹12,000 premium became ₹14,160 with GST
The same plan now costs ₹12,000 with a straight ₹2,160 saving in comparison to the pre GST2.0 era
Key Takeaways
1. Effective from September 22, 2025, GST on term insurance is now 0% for individual plans,
making premiums noticeably cheaper.
2. Policyholders get more value for the same budget, allowing higher cover without extra cost. 3. The reform supports national financial goals by improving access to basic life protection. 4. People from rural and semi-urban areas can buy a term insurance easily without tax liability. 5. Existing customers also benefit, as future premiums will automatically reduce.
Conclusion
The withdrawal of GST on term insurance is a big step in making financial protection more affordable for every Indian. It puts real savings back into your pocket and encourages more people to secure long-term cover. For insurers, it signals stronger growth and wider reach, bringing India’s goal of “Insurance for All” closer to reality. If you’ve been waiting to buy a term plan, this is the right moment to lock in low premiums and protect your family’s future.
FAQs
1. Is the GST change relevant for individual term plans and group term plans alike?
The GST waiver is limited to individual term insurance plans. Premiums of group term insurance plans will include 18% GST even under the GST 2.0 reform. 2. Can policyholders claim a GST refund on term insurance premiums already paid for
previous years? Refunds aren't allowed for GST that was charged on earlier payments3. The new GST exemption applies only when two out of three events: service period, invoice date, or payment date, fall on September 22, 2025 or after this date. If two of these events happened before that date, the old GST rate continues to apply2. 3. How to Calculate GST on Term Insurance?
Before GST 2.0, the premium amount of individual and group plans attracted 18% GST. This means that an annual premium of ₹10,000 attracted an additional tax of ₹1,800, bringing the total premium amount to ₹11,800. After the reform, a premium of ₹10,000 on individual term plan will not incur any extra GST charges.
